name: Can I switch my bathtub to a walk-in shower, and is it a good idea?
headline: Can I switch my bathtub to a walk-in shower, and is it a good idea?

mainEntity.name: Can I switch my bathtub to a walk-in shower, and is it a good idea?
mainEntity.text: Can I switch my bathtub to a walk-in shower, and is it a good idea?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you can usually change a tub to a walk-in shower during a remodel, and many homeowners in the Pacifica and Daly City area are doing exactly that. It can make the bathroom feel more open and improve accessibility, especially for aging in place. However, there are a few things to think about. From a resale perspective, it’s often smart to keep at least one bathtub somewhere in the house, because families with young children may want it. If your home has only one bathroom, consider whether a tub-shower combo might be better. Converting to a walk-in shower involves plumbing changes, waterproofing, and usually new tile. You’ll need a properly sloped shower pan or a pre-made base. Good drainage and waterproofing are critical to avoid leaks and mold. Plan for safety and comfort: a low or curbless entry, slip-resistant tile, a sturdy grab bar, and possibly a bench or ledge. Glass shower doors should swing out for safety and be sized for easy access. A common pitfall is choosing tiny floor tiles that are hard to clean or too smooth tile that becomes slippery when wet. Another mistake is placing the showerhead where it sprays directly at the door, causing water to escape. Work with your contractor on layout to keep water inside the shower and make the space both safe and attractive.
